We're going to go straight through the launch so I hope you peed and we're going to zip. By the way everybody have a good time last night? It was a little quick huh? I suspect there was red bulls before the one in hand that's that's what I suspect. Alright let's get into module 5 and let's talk about well let's start on 1.15 I have a couple things to say about that. They are I'll try and be quick here early because they are obvious and not new and yet you actually saw a little bit of it happen last night. I always say that and it's almost always true that in a consulting day with a client I can usually stump them in the first 20 minutes within the first five questions because there's more people don't know about their own business than they do know and and the more entrepreneurial by character they are the more sales by character or conditioning they are the more that's true because then what they tend not to know are all of the statistical and numerical things about their business. People who don't come from our world you can stump them with just cost per lead cost per sale customer value they don't know that stuff and for the most part they've never even thought about that stuff and all of the math that they do get in their business doesn't tell them that directly so what they're looking at are financial statements prepared by the accountant and tax returns and monthly bookkeeping and you know so forth and I mean it can be extrapolated out of there but but they don't do it. People who come from our world more often than not they have a handle on that although you'd be surprised how many people doing a lot of gross and who you would think are smart when pressed their handle on that is what they suspect not what they know and there's a huge difference between what somebody believes to be true about their business and what is actually true about their business but if you start to go deeper you find that most people aren't very aggressively benchmarking at all and so they're essentially trying to achieve a level of performance that they have not defined and cannot measure other than by the end outcome which by then it's too late to do anything about what has been occurring and so like a simple thing that relates to productivity is 90% of the world sets appointments and meetings with beginning times and no end times and so that alone tells you that every appointment every meeting is going to take longer than it needed to. I used to get paid to go to meetings and so a guthy rancor and quantum another big infomercial company these pretty big companies and all big companies have lots of meetings in fact the number of meetings and the number of minutes devoted to meetings and the number of people involved in meetings grows in a parallel line has the size of a company grows and gradually this line strangles that one and then we bail it out. So these lots of meetings and quantum's meetings were great. Dave Petito and Stan Jacobs and I love going to quantum's meetings because the CEO of quantum I mean the food was spectacular. I mean just spectacular and nobody wanted to leave because if you stayed long enough they brought in more food you know so I mean so I mean you started with breakfast and then in came this massive catered lunch and if you stayed long enough in came mid-afternoon snack and hell if you made the meeting go long enough they brought in dinner you know so I mean we're working on a show you know who Joan Quigley is there's a trivia question for you there's one of these people who was
